title: chaenactis alpigena
intro: Chaenactis alpigena, also known as the alpine buckwheat or white pygmy, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Chaenactis alpigena include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. Herbal preparations of Chaenactis alpigena include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, from skin irritations to respiratory infections.
